summary: 23 attributes across 4 tiers. NEW: 5 columns added (K–O) showing ingestion model, AI confidence, LLM technique, human role, and adjusted labeling time.

attributes.name: Document ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Dates (Submit / Required / Response)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Response Time (calendar days)
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic

attributes.name: Author / Assignee
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Drawing References
attributes.options: Sheet numbers parsed from body text
attributes.llmTechnique: Regex + LLM extraction of drawing sheet patterns (A-###, S-###, M-###, Detail #/X-###)

attributes.name: Spec Section References
attributes.options: Spec sections parsed from body text
attributes.llmTechnique: Regex for MasterFormat patterns (## ## ##) + LLM extraction of written references ('Section 07 62 00')

attributes.name: CSI Division (Primary)
attributes.labelerQualification: PE / APM with CSI knowledge (2+ yrs)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads RFI body + MasterFormat division list as context. 'Waterproofing membrane at parapet' → Div 07. Few-shot with 30 examples.

attributes.name: CSI Division (Secondary)
attributes.labelerQualification: PE / APM with CSI knowledge (2+ yrs)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies secondary trade mentions. 'Steel sub to modify clip' → Div 05. Harder when secondary is implicit.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Pre-filled → correct if wrong. ~5 sec/doc.

attributes.name: CSI Subdivision (6-digit)
attributes.labelerQualification: PE / APM with CSI knowledge (2+ yrs)
attributes.llmTechnique: Cascaded: if Div 07, map text to 6-digit using MasterFormat section table. 'Sheet metal flashing' → 07 62 00.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Depends on primary division accuracy.

attributes.name: Building System
attributes.options: Envelope | Structure | MEP Rough-In | MEP Finish | Interiors/Finishes | Site/Civil | Vertical Transport | Fire/Life Safety | Specialties | General Conditions
attributes.whyItMatters: System-level queries matching field team thinking
attributes.llmTechnique: 10-category classification. LLM maps RFI scope to system. Simple — 'parapet flashing' → Envelope, 'ductwork' → MEP Rough-In.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 5% only. Very high accuracy expected.

attributes.name: Trade(s) Responsible
attributes.example: Primary: ABC Waterproofing | Secondary: DEF Steel
attributes.whyItMatters: Sub performance tracking; RFI volume by sub
attributes.llmTechnique: NLP extraction of company names from RFI text + fuzzy match against client sub directory (Procore API). Handles abbreviations.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Verify when fuzzy match confidence <0.80.

attributes.name: Project Phase When Discovered
attributes.llmTechnique: Heuristic: RFI date vs. project start/end dates from Procore. Calc approx % complete. Cross-ref with schedule milestones if available.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Pre-filled from date heuristic. ~3 sec to confirm/correct.

attributes.name: Root Cause (Level 1)
attributes.options: Design Conflict | Missing Information | Field Condition | Code/Regulatory | Constructability | Coordination | Owner Change | Spec Clarification | Other
attributes.whyItMatters: THE most important label. Drives root cause analysis, cost attribution, preconstruction strategy.
attributes.llmTechnique: Few-shot prompt with all 9 category definitions + disambiguation rules + 30 labeled examples. Chain-of-thought reasoning stored with label. Key: Design Conflict vs. Missing Info distinction.
attributes.humanRole: VERIFY ALL. Pre-filled with reasoning visible. Human confirms or overrides. ~10–15 sec with AI pre-fill vs 30–45 from scratch.

attributes.name: Root Cause (Level 2)
attributes.options: Design Conflict: Arch vs. Structural | Arch vs. MEP | MEP vs. MEP | etc. Missing Info: Detail Not Provided | Section Not Addressed | etc. Field Condition: Subsurface | Existing Conditions | etc. Coordination: Spatial | Sequencing | Access
attributes.example: Arch vs. Structural — flashing elevation conflicts with beam/topping
attributes.whyItMatters: Specific failure mode identification; targeted precon checklists
attributes.llmTechnique: Cascaded prompt: given L1 = Design Conflict, classify L2 from filtered subcategories. Accuracy depends on L1 being correct. Chain carefully.
attributes.humanRole: VERIFY ALL. Depends on L1 accuracy — if L1 wrong, L2 wrong. Human reviews both together. ~8–10 sec.

attributes.name: Urgency
attributes.options: Critical (work stopped) | High (blocked within 5 days) | Standard (no immediate impact) | Low (informational/future phase)
attributes.example: High — sub mobilized, pour in 5 days
attributes.whyItMatters: Which RFI types create field emergencies
attributes.llmTechnique: Strong text signals: 'crew waiting', 'critical path', 'scheduled next week', 'mobilized', 'work stopped'. Pattern matching + LLM classification.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. False negatives (missing urgency) flagged for review.

attributes.name: Cost Impact (Estimated Range)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies scope-change signals and estimates magnitude from text. 'Revised detail + new clips + manufacturer coordination' → Moderate. Classification into ranges, not dollar estimates.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Especially Major/Critical — review those carefully. ~5–8 sec.

attributes.name: Schedule Impact (Estimated Range)
attributes.options: None | Minor (1–3 days) | Moderate (3–10 days) | Major (10–30 days) | Critical (>30 days / LD exposure)
attributes.whyItMatters: Schedule risk by root cause, trade, project type
attributes.llmTechnique: Text signals: 'critical path', 'delay', '5 days'. LLM estimates range. Less reliable — schedule context is external to the document.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Critical path determination needs field judgment. ~5–8 sec.

attributes.name: Resolution Outcome
attributes.options: No Change Required | ASI / Design Revision | Change Order Generated | Claim / Dispute | Deferred / Still Open
attributes.whyItMatters: Bridges RFI data to CO data
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads response text + checks for ASI/CO references. 'Revised detail attached (ASI-034)' → ASI. If no response → 'Still Open'. Clear text signals.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Very reliable when response is populated.

attributes.name: Preventability
attributes.options: Yes-Likely (catchable in precon) | Yes-Possibly (enhanced review) | No (unforeseeable/owner-driven)
attributes.example: Yes-Likely — flashing/structural conflict visible on drawings during precon review
attributes.whyItMatters: THE highest-value label. Quantifies preventable cost of poor coordination.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM generates suggestion with reasoning based on root cause + description. Suggestion shown to human but NOT pre-selected. Human makes final call.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows reasoning but human selects. Every correction logged with justification for training. ~20 sec.

attributes.name: Responsibility Attribution
attributes.options: Design Team | Owner | GC Internal | Subcontractor | Regulatory/Code | Shared/Unclear
attributes.example: Design Team — architect's detail didn't account for structural elevation
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM suggests based on root cause + text analysis. 'Per ASI from architect' → Design Team. But politically sensitive — context matters beyond text.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI suggestion visible but human decides. Used for architect/owner benchmarking — errors damage relationships.

attributes.name: Recurring Pattern
attributes.options: First Instance | Recurring-Same Project | Recurring-Cross Project | Known Industry Issue
attributes.example: Recurring-Cross Project — 3 of last 5 healthcare projects had parapet/structural conflicts
attributes.whyItMatters: Predictive engine; 'what to watch for' on new projects
attributes.llmTechnique: After 2000+ labeled RFIs: system runs semantic similarity search against labeled corpus and flags potential matches. Before that: human knowledge only.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION initially. After corpus builds, system suggests similar RFIs — human confirms pattern. Evolves toward AI+Verify at Month 12+.

attributes.name: Related Documents
attributes.options: RFI numbers, CO numbers, ASI numbers from same project or cross-project
attributes.llmTechnique: Regex + LLM extraction of explicit doc references (RFI-###, ASI-###, CO-###). High accuracy on explicit refs. Implicit relationships (same issue, different doc) via semantic search.
attributes.humanRole: Verify explicit refs (fast). Add implicit relationships human recognizes. ~8–10 sec.

summary: 14 attributes per clause. Highest-expertise labeling. AI assists with classification + AIA comparison; human decides on risk scoring, negotiability, and recommended actions.

attributes.name: Contract ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull from source system

attributes.name: Contract Type
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from document structure + title. 'Subcontract Agreement between...' → GC-Sub.

attributes.name: Parties
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts party names from preamble/signature block. Fuzzy match to Procore sub directory.

attributes.name: Contract Value
attributes.options: Dollar amount from contract or Sage
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts dollar amount from contract. Cross-ref with Sage committed cost for validation.
attributes.humanRole: None — flag mismatches between contract and Sage

attributes.name: Clause Section Reference
attributes.llmTechnique: Regex + LLM extraction of 'Article X.X.X' or 'Section ##' patterns from clause text. Well-structured in most contracts.

attributes.name: Clause Category (12 types)
attributes.options: Indemnification | Liquidated Damages | No-Damage-for-Delay | Payment Terms | Change Order Procedures | Termination | Consequential Damages | Insurance Requirements | Dispute Resolution | Warranty | Flow-Down Provisions | Scope Definition
attributes.llmTechnique: 12 categories with linguistically distinct language. Few-shot with 3 examples per category. 'Liquidated damages at the rate of...' → LD. Very reliable.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Categories are distinctive enough for high accuracy.

attributes.name: Risk Direction
attributes.options: Risk to GC | Risk to Owner | Risk to Sub | Shared/Balanced | Risk to Architect
attributes.example: Risk to GC — 14-day notice period is short
attributes.whyItMatters: Enables 'all clauses where GC bears disproportionate risk'
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies who bears risk from clause language. 'Contractor shall indemnify' → Risk to GC. 'Shared/Balanced' vs. 'Risk to GC' harder.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI pre-fills; attorney/PM confirms. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Risk Score (1–5)
attributes.options: 1 = Favorable to GC | 2 = Standard | 3 = Moderate risk | 4 = Elevated | 5 = Unacceptable / requires negotiation
attributes.labelerQualification: Construction attorney (5+ yrs) or Sr. PM (10+ yrs)
attributes.example: 4 — 'materially different' subjective; 14-day notice shorter than AIA
attributes.whyItMatters: Core risk metric; aggregates to contract-level score
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM provides AIA baseline comparison and suggests score with reasoning. Suggestion shown but NOT pre-selected. Stakes too high for auto-fill.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows AIA comparison + suggested reasoning. Attorney/PM scores independently. Every correction logged.

attributes.name: Deviation from Standard
attributes.options: More Favorable than AIA | AIA Standard | Minor Deviation | Major Deviation | Non-Standard (custom)
attributes.example: Major Deviation — Notice shortened 21→14 days; new 'materially different' language vs. AIA A201 §4.3
attributes.whyItMatters: Quantifies how far from standard; identifies aggressive owners/architects
attributes.llmTechnique: CRITICAL TECHNIQUE: Feed LLM the actual clause + corresponding AIA A201 standard clause side by side. LLM DIFFS them and classifies deviation level. Specific changes highlighted.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI comparison is a strong starting point. Attorney reviews diff and confirms/adjusts. ~15 sec.

attributes.name: Recommended Contingency %
attributes.example: 1–2% — build contingency for concealed conditions disputes
attributes.whyItMatters: Directly feeds estimating; connects risk to bid strategy
attributes.llmTechnique: After 200+ labeled clauses: system can suggest based on historical contingency for similar clause types. Before that: no reliable basis.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Directly impacts bids. Wrong number = underbid. Senior expertise required.

attributes.name: Negotiability Assessment
attributes.options: Always Negotiable | Usually Negotiable | Sometimes | Rarely | Non-Negotiable (statutory/bonding)
attributes.example: Usually Negotiable — notice periods are commonly negotiated upward
attributes.whyItMatters: Saves PM political capital; focuses on winnable clauses
attributes.llmTechnique: After corpus builds: system suggests based on historical outcomes with same owner. 'Owner X has negotiated this clause on 3 of 4 prior contracts.' Before data: human only.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Requires relationship knowledge + market experience. No text signal for this.

attributes.name: Historical Dispute Flag
attributes.options: Yes-This Firm (link to dispute) | Yes-Industry Common | No Known Disputes | Unknown
attributes.labelerQualification: Sr. PM (10+ yrs) or construction attorney
attributes.example: Yes-This Firm — $180K dispute on Project #2022-018 over similar language
attributes.whyItMatters: Evidence-based risk scoring; opinion → track record
attributes.llmTechnique: After labeled dispute data exists: system auto-flags SIMILAR clauses in new contracts. Semantic similarity search against dispute-linked clauses. Evolves toward AI+Verify at Month 12+.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION initially. System provides 'similar clause in Contract X resulted in dispute' after corpus builds.

attributes.name: Recommended Action
attributes.options: Accept As-Is | Negotiate (with suggested language) | Add Contingency | Flag for Legal | Reject/Walk
attributes.example: Negotiate: Request 21-day notice per AIA. If refused, add 1.5% contingency.
attributes.whyItMatters: Actionable guidance — the 'so what' of contract scoring
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM drafts suggestion based on risk score + deviation analysis. Shown as 'AI draft' for human to edit — NEVER auto-populated. Highest liability risk.
attributes.humanRole: FULL AUTHORSHIP. AI draft shown for inspiration only. Human writes final recommendation. Every recommendation logged.

attributes.name: Clause Risk Trend
attributes.options: Getting Worse | Stable | Getting Better | New Clause Type | Insufficient History
attributes.example: Stable — this owner has used similar language on last 3 contracts
attributes.whyItMatters: Identifies owners whose terms are tightening
attributes.llmTechnique: Requires prior labeled contracts with same owner. LLM compares clause language across contract versions. 'LD rate increased from $2K/day to $5K/day across last 3 contracts' → Getting Worse.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Only works after first engagement. System feature, not pure labeling.

summary: 18 attributes. Financial backbone — bridges RFI intelligence to cost outcomes. AI handles classification and document chain; human decides on strategy, preventability, and lessons learned.

attributes.name: CO Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Dates (Initiated / Approved / Closed)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: GC Proposed Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: API pull or LLM extraction from CO document

attributes.name: Owner Approved Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: API pull

attributes.name: Negotiation Delta ($)
attributes.llmTechnique: Simple arithmetic

attributes.name: CSI Division(s)
attributes.llmTechnique: Same as RFI: LLM reads CO scope description + MasterFormat. CO descriptions tend to be clearer than RFIs. Multi-division slightly harder.

attributes.name: Building System
attributes.llmTechnique: 10-category classification. Same as RFI. Even easier on COs — scope descriptions are explicit.

attributes.name: Initiating Party
attributes.options: Owner | Architect/Engineer | GC (self-identified) | Subcontractor | Regulatory/Code | Field Condition
attributes.llmTechnique: Strong text signals: 'Owner-directed', 'per ASI-034', 'field condition discovered during excavation'. 6 categories, linguistically distinct.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Usually explicit in CO documentation.

attributes.name: Change Reason (Root Cause)
attributes.options: Design Error/Omission | Owner-Directed | Unforeseen Condition | Code Change | Value Engineering | Contractor Error | Scope Gap/Ambiguity | Schedule Acceleration
attributes.whyItMatters: Links to RFI root cause for end-to-end analysis
attributes.llmTechnique: 8 root cause categories. Easier than RFI root cause — COs written AFTER issue is understood. Retrospective clarity helps. Few-shot with 20 examples.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Pre-filled → confirm or correct. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Schedule Impact (Days)
attributes.whyItMatters: Schedule cost of changes; delay attribution
attributes.llmTechnique: Day count extraction from CO text: 'Request 5 additional calendar days.' High accuracy on explicit statements. Critical path flag needs schedule context.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Day count usually correct; critical path needs human judgment. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Markup Applied
attributes.options: Standard (10/10/10) | Below Standard | Above Standard | T&M | Lump Sum (no visible markup)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies markup structure from CO pricing breakdown. '10% OH, 10% Profit' → Standard. Requires pricing detail in CO document.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Lump sum COs without breakdown are harder. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Backup Documentation Quality
attributes.options: Strong (3+ docs) | Adequate (1–2 docs) | Weak (verbal/email only) | None
attributes.example: Strong — RFI, ASI, sub proposals, photos
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM counts supporting document references in CO package: sub proposals? Photos? RFIs? ASIs? Count = quality proxy. 3+ = Strong.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Good heuristic — human confirms count-based classification. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Originating Document Chain
attributes.options: Linked RFIs → ASIs → CO in causal chain
attributes.llmTechnique: NLP extraction of referenced RFIs, ASIs, CCDs from CO text. Build chain programmatically: 'per RFI-0247 and ASI-034'. Explicit refs high accuracy.
attributes.humanRole: Verify chain completeness. AI catches explicit refs; human adds implicit links. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Disputed (Y/N) + Outcome
attributes.options: Not Disputed | Disputed-Resolved Favorably | Disputed-Compromised | Disputed-Lost | Disputed-Pending | Escalated to Claim
attributes.whyItMatters: Dispute probability by root cause, owner, contract type
attributes.llmTechnique: Combine Procore status field (Approved/Rejected/Pending) + text signals ('disputed', 'rejected', 'counter-proposal'). Y/N reliable; outcome nuance needs human.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Dispute flag reliable from status. Outcome classification needs human for nuance. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Negotiation Strategy Used
attributes.options: Historical Precedent | Spec/Contract Reference | Sub Proposals | T&M Records | Photos/Video | Schedule Impact Leverage | Third-Party Estimate | None/Standard
attributes.whyItMatters: Which strategies correlate with best outcomes
attributes.llmTechnique: Requires reading negotiation context — emails, meeting notes, back-and-forth. Not in CO text alone. AI can flag some references but can't assess strategy.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Most valuable intelligence label. AI can't read negotiation dynamics.

attributes.name: Preventability
attributes.example: Yes-Likely — coordination review would have caught drawing conflict
attributes.whyItMatters: Total preventable change cost across portfolio
attributes.llmTechnique: Same as RFI preventability. LLM suggests with reasoning but accuracy too low to trust for financial metrics.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI suggestion visible. Human decides. Directly feeds 'cost of preventable changes' metric.

attributes.name: Lessons Learned / Precon Flag
attributes.options: Free text note + Flag for Precon Checklist: Y/N
attributes.example: Flag: Y — 'Verify parapet/structural clearances at design review on all healthcare projects'
attributes.whyItMatters: Preconstruction checklist that gets smarter per project
attributes.llmTechnique: After corpus builds: AI drafts suggestion based on root cause + project type patterns from prior labeled COs. Draft shown for human to edit.
attributes.humanRole: FULL AUTHORSHIP. AI draft for inspiration. Human writes actionable insight. The compound intelligence.

summary: 41 attributes across ASIs, CCDs, PRs/PCOs/CORs, and Bulletins. The full change management ecosystem with pipeline velocity tracking.

attributes.name: ASI Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Date Issued
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Issued By (Design Firm)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull + directory match

attributes.name: CSI Division(s) Affected
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads ASI description + MasterFormat. Same technique as RFI/CO. ASIs have clear scope descriptions.

attributes.name: Building System
attributes.options: Envelope | Structure | MEP | Interiors | Site | etc.
attributes.llmTechnique: 10-category classification. Same as all other schemas. Very reliable.

attributes.name: ASI Type
attributes.options: Design Correction | Design Enhancement | Coordination Resolution | Code Compliance | Owner-Directed | Value Engineering | Clarification Only
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from ASI description. 'Revised per coordination with structural' → Coordination Resolution. 'Per owner request' → Owner-Directed.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Important for design team accountability. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Cost Impact
attributes.options: No Cost | Additive | Deductive | Net Zero | TBD
attributes.example: Additive — revised detail adds clip coordination
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies cost signals. 'Additional work' → Additive. 'Clarification only, no change to scope' → No Cost.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Important for cost attribution. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Estimated Cost Range
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM estimates magnitude from scope description. Range classification, not dollar estimate.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Cost estimation is inherently uncertain. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Schedule Impact
attributes.options: None | Minor (1-5 days) | Moderate (5-15 days) | Major (>15 days) | Unknown
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies schedule signals. Needs project context for accuracy.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Schedule impact assessment requires project knowledge. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Triggering Document
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts RFI references from ASI text. 'In response to RFI-0247' → link. If no RFI mentioned → 'Architect-Initiated.'
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Explicit references high accuracy. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Resulting PR/CO
attributes.options: PR or CO generated from ASI
attributes.whyItMatters: Connects design decision to financial outcome
attributes.llmTechnique: System searches PRs/COs referencing this ASI number. Auto-links.

attributes.name: Rework Required?
attributes.example: No — ASI received before waterproofing began
attributes.whyItMatters: Rework cost of late design decisions
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM compares ASI date to project schedule/daily reports. If work was in progress → Yes. If not started → No.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Requires knowing if work was already installed. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Preventability
attributes.example: Yes-Likely — visible on drawings during precon
attributes.llmTechnique: Same as RFI/CO. LLM suggests; human decides.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. High-value label for quantifying preventable change cost.

attributes.name: CCD Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Date Issued / Date Effective
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts dates from CCD document.

attributes.name: Issued By
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads CCD header/signature. 'Directed by Owner' → Owner. Very explicit in formal documents.

attributes.name: CSI Divisions Affected
attributes.llmTechnique: Same as all other schemas.

attributes.name: CCD Reason
attributes.options: Owner Program Change | Owner Upgrade/Downgrade | Code Change | Unforeseen Condition | Design Error Resolution | Schedule Acceleration | Third-Party Requirement
attributes.example: Owner Program Change — additional medical gas outlets
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from CCD description. 'Owner requests additional...' → Owner Program Change.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Important for owner behavior analytics. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: GC Proposed Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts dollar amount from CCD response/pricing. Usually stated explicitly.

attributes.name: Final Approved Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: Same extraction from approved CO.

attributes.name: Days CCD to Price Agreement
attributes.example: 34 days at risk before price locked
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic.

attributes.name: Disputed?
attributes.options: Not Disputed | Disputed-Price | Disputed-Scope | Disputed-Schedule | Escalated
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies dispute signals from correspondence/CO text. 'Rejected', 'counter-offer', 'dispute'.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Dispute detection from text is reliable. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Resulting CO
attributes.options: CO this CCD was rolled into
attributes.llmTechnique: System matches CCD to CO by project + scope + date range. Usually explicit link.

attributes.name: Document Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Document Type
attributes.options: PR (owner/architect asks GC for pricing) | PCO (GC-initiated) | COR (sub-initiated) | Bulletin
attributes.llmTechnique: Clear type identification from document title/header. 'Proposal Request' → PR. Bulletin format distinctive.

attributes.name: Originating Event
attributes.options: What triggered: RFI | ASI | CCD | Field Condition | Owner Request | Sub Request | Code | VE
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts references: 'per ASI-034', 'in response to field condition'. Maps to trigger categories.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Explicit references reliable; implicit triggers need confirmation. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Date Submitted
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Date Approved/Rejected/Pending
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Pipeline Duration (days)
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic.

attributes.name: CSI Division(s)
attributes.llmTechnique: Same as all other schemas.

attributes.name: Proposed Amount
attributes.whyItMatters: Links to CO for negotiation delta
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts amount from PR/PCO. Usually clearly stated.

attributes.name: Approval Status
attributes.options: Approved | Approved-Reduced | Rejected | Withdrawn | Pending-Owner | Pending-Architect | Pending-GC | Bundled
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from Procore status + resolution text. 'Approved at reduced amount' → Approved-Reduced.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Nuance between Pending-Owner vs Pending-Architect matters. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Stall Point (if delayed)
attributes.options: No Delay | Stalled at Owner | Architect | GC Pricing | Sub Pricing | Negotiation | Bundling Delay
attributes.example: Stalled at Owner — 12 of 21 days were owner review
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can analyze date gaps in approval chain if status history is available. But root cause of stall requires project knowledge.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows timeline. Human identifies bottleneck from experience.

attributes.name: Resulting CO Number
attributes.options: Final CO this was rolled into
attributes.llmTechnique: System matches PR/PCO to CO by project + amount + date. Usually explicit.

attributes.name: Conversion Rate Flag
attributes.options: Did this become approved CO? Y/N + recovery rate
attributes.llmTechnique: Auto-calc from linked CO data.

attributes.name: Document Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Issued By
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from document header.

attributes.name: Classification
attributes.options: Clarification Only | Minor Revision | Change to Contract Scope | Scope Creep (disguised as clarification) | Design Correction
attributes.example: Change to Contract Scope — revised flashing changes installed conditions
attributes.whyItMatters: THE critical classification. Scope creep detection.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM compares bulletin content to original contract scope. 'Revised detail' with new conditions → Change. 'Clarification consistent with design intent' → Clarification.
attributes.humanRole: VERIFY ALL CAREFULLY. This is where missed revenue lives. AI flags potential scope changes; human decides. ~10 sec.

attributes.name: PR/CO Generated?
attributes.options: Yes (link) | No — Should Have Been (missed revenue) | No — Not a Change | Under Review
attributes.llmTechnique: System checks if a PR/CO references this bulletin. If Classification = 'Change to Scope' but no linked PR → flag 'Should Have Been.'
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI identifies the gap; human confirms it's truly missed revenue. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Estimated Missed Revenue
attributes.options: If not priced, estimated value of missed change
attributes.llmTechnique: Only applies when Classification = 'Should Have Been.' Requires scope assessment to estimate value.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Senior expertise to estimate scope value.

summary: 33 attributes. Bid-level: cost benchmarking, win/loss analytics, bid strategy. One label set per estimate. Post-completion labels close the feedback loop.

attributes.name: Estimate ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Project Name / Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Bid Date
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Estimator(s)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Total Bid Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Bid Result
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Contract Amount (if won)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Final Project Cost (if complete)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Project Type
attributes.options: Healthcare | K-12 | Higher Ed | Municipal | Multifamily | Commercial | Retail | Industrial | Mixed-Use | Religious | Hospitality | Senior Living
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads project name + description + bid documents. 'Main St Medical Center' → Healthcare. Cross-ref Procore project type field.

attributes.name: Project Subtype
attributes.options: Healthcare: Acute Care | MOB | Ambulatory | Behavioral K-12: Elementary | Middle | High | Athletic Higher Ed: Academic | Housing | Research | Athletic
attributes.llmTechnique: Cascaded: given Healthcare, classify subtype from description. 'Medical center' vs 'medical office building' distinction matters.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI pre-fills; estimator confirms. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Building Type
attributes.options: New Construction | Major Renovation | Addition | Tenant Improvement | Adaptive Reuse | Shell & Core | Phased (occupied)
attributes.whyItMatters: Reno vs new = different cost profile
attributes.llmTechnique: Usually stated in project description or bid documents. 'New 3-story medical center' → New Construction. 'Renovation of existing wing' → Major Renovation.

attributes.name: Gross Square Footage
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from bid cover sheet or proposal. 'Approximately 82,000 gross square feet.' Also available from estimating system in many cases.

attributes.name: Number of Stories
attributes.llmTechnique: Extract from project description. '3-story building with basement' → 3+1. Simple text pattern.

attributes.name: Delivery Method
attributes.options: Hard Bid | GMP | CM-at-Risk | Design-Build | CMAR | Cost-Plus | Negotiated
attributes.llmTechnique: Usually stated in bid invitation or contract. 'GMP proposal' → GMP. Very explicit in bid documents.

attributes.name: Owner Type
attributes.options: Public — State/County/City | Public — School District | Public — Federal | Private — Institutional | Private — Developer | Private — Corporate | Private — Non-Profit
attributes.whyItMatters: Public vs private changes wages, bonding, procurement
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from owner name + project description. 'Austin ISD' → Public-School District. Hospital system → Private-Institutional.

attributes.name: Geographic Market
attributes.llmTechnique: Extract from project address. Geocode to metro area. Very reliable.

attributes.name: Design Completeness at Bid
attributes.options: 100% CD | 90% CD | SD/DD | Bridging | Conceptual / Budget Only
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM looks for signals: 'SD/DD estimate', 'documents 90% complete', 'conceptual budget.' Often stated in bid cover letter.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. If not explicitly stated, estimator assesses. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Total Cost per SF (Bid)
attributes.llmTechnique: Simple division once GSF and bid are known.

attributes.name: Cost Breakdown by Division
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from estimate summary or bid tabulation spreadsheet. Maps line items to CSI divisions. Most time-consuming extraction.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Pre-extracted table; estimator confirms mapping. Saves ~50% vs manual. ~60–90 sec.

attributes.name: Contingency Applied
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts contingency line from estimate summary. Amount and percentage usually stated. Type classification from context.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Amount extraction reliable; type classification needs confirmation. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Escalation Assumptions
attributes.example: 4.5% annual, 18 months. Based on ENR CCI + local adjustment.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from estimate assumptions page. '4.5% escalation over 18 months' → structured extraction. Basis often stated.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Extraction reliable when stated; sometimes buried in notes. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Fee / Markup Structure
attributes.inputType: Table: Fee % | OH % | Insurance % | Bond % | Total
attributes.example: GC Fee: 4.0% | OH: 2.5% | Insurance: 1.8% | Bond: 1.2% | Total: 9.5%
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from estimate summary or fee proposal. Percentage breakdowns usually tabulated.

attributes.name: Labor Rate Basis
attributes.options: Prevailing Wage | Union | Open Shop | Blended | N/A (sub bids only)
attributes.example: Prevailing Wage — Travis County rates, Jan 2024
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM checks for prevailing wage / Davis-Bacon mentions. Public projects usually prevailing wage. 'Union scale' → Union.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Usually determinable from project type (public = prevailing wage). ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Sub Bids: Solicited vs Received
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from bid tabulation sheet. Column mapping from spreadsheet data. Spread auto-calculated.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Extraction from structured tabulations; mapping needs confirmation. ~30 sec.

attributes.name: Estimator Confidence Level
attributes.example: Moderate — strong structural, MEP based on 2 bids only
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can assess sub bid coverage + design completeness as proxy. But true confidence is the estimator's judgment call.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows bid coverage metrics as context. Estimator assigns confidence.

attributes.name: Market Condition at Bid
attributes.options: Very Hot | Hot | Normal | Soft | Recessionary
attributes.example: Hot — Austin market Jan 2024, healthcare demand high
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM could pull market indicators (ENR CCI, permit data) as context. But assessment is subjective and local.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. System provides market data as context. Human characterizes conditions.

attributes.name: Key Assumptions & Exclusions
attributes.example: Assumes: single shift, unobstructed access. Excludes: owner-furnished medical equip ($2.1M)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from estimate cover letter / assumptions page. 'Excludes:', 'Assumes:', 'Not included:' are clear text markers.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Extraction from assumptions sections is reliable. Completeness check by human. ~15 sec.

attributes.name: Scope Coverage Assessment
attributes.options: Fully Covered | Partially Covered | Excluded | Allowance Only
attributes.example: Div 03: Fully Covered | Div 22-23: Partially (2 bids, no coord dwgs)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can flag divisions with few sub bids or 'allowance' language. But full scope assessment requires estimating expertise.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI flags potential gaps (low bid coverage, 'allowance' mentions). Human assesses each division.

attributes.name: Win/Loss Analysis Notes
attributes.options: If lost: Reason + known winning price If won: Why selected
attributes.example: Won — Best Value. Low bidder $13.8M but owner had concerns about healthcare experience.
attributes.whyItMatters: Win rate by factor; pricing strategy
attributes.llmTechnique: If lost, LLM can extract competitor pricing from debriefs if documented. But selection rationale is relationship knowledge.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Institutional knowledge about why you won or lost. AI can structure the entry format.

attributes.name: Final Cost by Division
attributes.options: Mapped from Sage cost codes to CSI
attributes.llmTechnique: Sage cost codes mapped to CSI divisions. Mapping table built during onboarding. Auto-pulls actuals and calculates variance.
attributes.humanRole: Verify mapping. Cost code → CSI mapping is client-specific. Once validated, runs automatically. ~60 sec.

attributes.name: Variance Explanation by Division
attributes.inputType: Dropdown per division: Scope Change | Market | Quantity | Productivity | Sub Performance | Estimating Error | Unforeseen | Multiple
attributes.example: Div 22-23: +$370K → Scope Change (design): $180K, Productivity: $120K, Sub Performance: $70K
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can cross-reference COs attributed to each division (from CO labels) as starting context. But full attribution requires judgment.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI provides CO data and RFI data per division as context. Human explains the variance.

attributes.name: Overall Estimating Accuracy Score
attributes.whyItMatters: Portfolio accuracy by estimator, type, method
attributes.llmTechnique: Auto-calculated from labeled data once final cost and owner-directed CO exclusions are in place.

attributes.name: Lessons Learned for Future Bids
attributes.options: Takeaway + Flag for Estimating Checklist: Y/N + Project Types
attributes.example: Flag: Y — 'On healthcare with incomplete MEP docs, add 8-10% contingency to Div 22-23 instead of 5%.'
attributes.whyItMatters: THE compound interest of the system
attributes.llmTechnique: After 10+ completed projects: AI drafts suggestions based on variance patterns. 'Div 22-23 has averaged +9.2% on healthcare. Consider increasing contingency.'
attributes.humanRole: FULL AUTHORSHIP. AI provides variance data and patterns. Human writes actionable insight.

summary: 29 attributes. Bid package level + individual sub bid level + post-completion outcomes. The buyout intelligence engine.

attributes.name: Bid Package ID
attributes.whyItMatters: Links all sub bids for this scope
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct system pull

attributes.name: CSI Division(s)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads bid package title/description. 'Mechanical — plumbing and HVAC' → Div 22 + 23. Very reliable from package titles.

attributes.name: Scope Description
attributes.example: Complete mechanical — plumbing, HVAC, controls, T&B. Excludes fire protection.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM summarizes from bid invitation or scope document. Extracts inclusions/exclusions.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI summarizes; estimator confirms completeness. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Bid Package Value (Budget)
attributes.options: GC internal budget for this scope
attributes.llmTechnique: Pull from estimate cost breakdown (Estimate Schema Attribute #19) for matching divisions.

attributes.name: Number of Subs Solicited
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from bid log or invitation list if digitized. Count of invited subs.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. If bid log is structured, highly reliable. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Number of Bids Received
attributes.llmTechnique: Count from bid tabulation. Auto-calculated from received bids.

attributes.name: Bid Spread (Low to High)
attributes.llmTechnique: Auto-calc from bid amounts.

attributes.name: Subcontractor Name
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct from bid tab. Match to Procore directory.

attributes.name: Bid Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct from bid tab.

attributes.name: Bid Rank
attributes.llmTechnique: Sorted rank.

attributes.name: Variance from GC Budget
attributes.llmTechnique: Division.

attributes.name: Scope Exceptions / Qualifications
attributes.example: Major — Excludes controls wiring ($120K), excludes seismic bracing per spec 15073
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads bid qualifications/exclusions page. Identifies scope gaps. Flags 'excludes', 'does not include', 'by others' language.
attributes.humanRole: VERIFY ALL CAREFULLY. AI flags exclusion language; estimator assesses materiality and cost impact. ~20 sec.

attributes.name: Qualification Assessment
attributes.options: Fully Qualified | Qualified-Reservations | Underqualified | New to Firm | Preferred Sub
attributes.example: Qualified with Reservations — no project >$2M with HCB before
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can pull prior project history from Cortex if available. But assessment requires relationship knowledge and reference checking.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI provides performance history if available. Human assesses.

attributes.name: Prior Work History
attributes.options: None | 1–2 projects | 3–5 | 6+ | + performance summary from Cortex
attributes.example: 3 projects — CO rate: 4.2%, RFI volume: moderate, Punch: average
attributes.llmTechnique: System auto-pulls sub history from Cortex labeled data: project count, CO rate, performance score. Formats summary.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Auto-populated from labeled data. Human confirms accuracy and adds context. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Selected Sub
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct from contract records.

attributes.name: Selection Basis
attributes.options: Low Bid | Low Qualified | Best Value | Negotiated | Sole Source | Relationship/Repeat
attributes.example: Low Qualified Bid — lowest among 3 with hospital experience
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can infer if lowest bidder was selected (Low Bid) or not. But distinction between 'Best Value' and 'Negotiated' requires context.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Selection rationale is institutional knowledge.

attributes.name: Subcontract Amount
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct from Sage.

attributes.name: Bid-to-Contract Delta
attributes.llmTechnique: Subtraction.

attributes.name: Buyout Savings vs Budget
attributes.llmTechnique: Division.

attributes.name: Final Sub Cost (incl COs)
attributes.whyItMatters: Was the low bid really cheap?
attributes.llmTechnique: Sage total cost for this subcontract.

attributes.name: Total CO Value from Sub
attributes.options: Sum of COs attributed to sub's scope
attributes.whyItMatters: CO rate by sub, trade, bid position
attributes.llmTechnique: System pulls COs from Procore/Sage matching this sub and project. Sums. May need manual attribution for shared COs.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Auto-summed; human confirms attribution on shared COs. ~10 sec.

attributes.name: CO Rate vs Bid Position
attributes.options: CO % for low bidder vs 2nd/3rd across portfolio
attributes.example: Low bidder: 14.9%. Portfolio avg low on healthcare: 12.3%. Non-low: 6.8%.
attributes.llmTechnique: Cross-portfolio calculation from labeled data.

attributes.name: RFIs from Sub's Scope
attributes.options: Count of RFIs where sub's trade is responsible
attributes.example: 34 RFIs — 2.1× portfolio average for this trade/size
attributes.llmTechnique: System counts RFIs from RFI labels where Trade Responsible matches this sub. Auto-compared to portfolio average.
attributes.humanRole: Verify count. Some RFIs may span multiple trades. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Schedule Performance
attributes.options: Ahead | On Schedule | Minor Delays | Significant | Critical | Caused Delays to Others
attributes.example: Significant Delays — mechanical 3 weeks behind, delayed drywall in Bldg B
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can analyze schedule variance from P6 data if available. But 'caused delays to others' requires project knowledge.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. System provides schedule data. Human assesses impact and root cause.

attributes.name: Safety Record
attributes.options: Excellent | Good | Below Average | Poor | Insufficient Data
attributes.llmTechnique: System pulls safety incidents attributed to this sub from Safety schema. Auto-scores based on recordable rate + observation count.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Auto-scored from labeled safety data. Human confirms context. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Punch List Performance
attributes.options: Excellent (<10) | Average (10–30) | Below Average (30–60) | Poor (60+) | Back-charged
attributes.example: Below Average — 47 items, 3 weeks to close, 2 re-inspections
attributes.llmTechnique: System pulls punch item count from Procore for this sub's scope. Auto-categorizes by count threshold.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Count auto-pulled; human confirms attribution and assesses response time. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Overall Sub Performance Score
attributes.options: CO Rate (25%) + RFI Rate (15%) + Schedule (25%) + Safety (15%) + Punch (10%) + Responsiveness (10%) → 1–5
attributes.llmTechnique: Weighted composite from labeled data.

attributes.name: Would Use Again?
attributes.options: Yes-Unreservedly | Yes-Certain Types | Yes-With Conditions | No-Performance | No-Relationship | Insufficient Data
attributes.example: Yes-With Conditions: OK for K-12, NOT healthcare without verified hospital experience
attributes.llmTechnique: AI shows performance scorecard as context. But 'would use again' is a human judgment incorporating relationship dynamics.
attributes.humanRole: FULL AUTHORSHIP. System provides data. Human makes recommendation with conditions. Captures institutional knowledge.

summary: 18 attributes. Submittal review cycles, procurement criticality, and reviewer performance. Most fields are highly automatable.

attributes.name: Submittal Number
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Submittal Title / Description
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Submitted By (Sub)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Submitted To (Reviewer)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Dates (Submitted/Required/Returned)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Review Cycle Time (days)
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic

attributes.name: Status / Disposition
attributes.options: Approved | Approved as Noted | Revise & Resubmit | Rejected | For Record Only
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: CSI Division / Spec Section
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads submittal title/description + MasterFormat lookup. 'Concrete Mix Designs' → Div 03. Very reliable for submittals.

attributes.name: Building System
attributes.options: Envelope | Structure | MEP | Interiors | Site | etc.
attributes.llmTechnique: 10-category. Submittal titles are descriptive. 'Structural steel shop drawings' → Structure.

attributes.name: Submittal Type
attributes.options: Shop Drawing | Product Data | Sample | Mock-Up | Mix Design | Test Report | Certificate | Calculation | O&M Manual | Warranty
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from title + description. 'Concrete Mix Designs' → Mix Design. '1/4-scale mock-up' → Mock-Up. Distinctive language.

attributes.name: Procurement Critical?
attributes.options: Yes — Long Lead | Yes — Fabrication | No — Standard | No — Already Procured | For Record Only
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM flags based on submittal type + trade. Steel shop drawings → Yes-Fabrication. Mix designs → Yes-Long Lead. Heuristic by type.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Procurement context is project-specific. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Schedule Impact of Late Review
attributes.options: No Impact | Minor (1-5 days) | Moderate (5-15 days) | Critical (>15 days) | Already Delayed
attributes.example: Moderate — 11-day late return pushes procurement 1 week
attributes.llmTechnique: Calculate from review cycle time vs. required date. If 11 days late → flag. Severity needs procurement context.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI flags lateness; human assesses schedule impact. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Resubmittal Count
attributes.llmTechnique: Procore tracks this natively

attributes.name: Resubmittal Reason (if applicable)
attributes.options: N/A | Sub Error | Incomplete | Reviewer Requested Alternates | Spec Disagreement | Coordination Issue | Changed Conditions
attributes.llmTechnique: If resubmittal exists, LLM reads reviewer comments to classify reason. 'Revise per spec section 03 30 00' → Spec Disagreement.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. LLM reads review comments well but nuance varies. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Reviewer Quality
attributes.options: Thorough | Standard | Rubber Stamp (risk) | Inconsistent | Obstructive (unreasonable delays)
attributes.example: Thorough — specific comments on mix proportions
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can assess comment length and specificity as proxy. But 'Rubber Stamp' vs 'Standard' requires knowing what thorough review looks like.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI can flag no-comment approvals as potential 'Rubber Stamp'. Human decides.

attributes.name: Related RFIs
attributes.llmTechnique: Search for RFIs mentioning this submittal number or same spec section within ±30 days. Semantic match.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI suggests matches; human confirms relevance. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Lessons Learned
attributes.options: Actionable insight + Flag for Precon Checklist: Y/N
attributes.llmTechnique: After corpus: AI flags patterns like 'Div 08 curtain wall submittals avg 3.2 resubmittals on healthcare.' Before corpus: human only.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Pattern recognition requires cross-project experience.

summary: 12 attributes. Structured extraction from superintendent narratives. LLMs are strong at NLP extraction from unstructured text — this is a core strength.

attributes.name: Report ID / Date
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Author (Superintendent)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Weather Conditions
attributes.llmTechnique: Procore fields + weather API backfill for missing data

attributes.name: Crews on Site (Structured)
attributes.options: Extract from narrative into structured rows
attributes.example: Concrete: Smith Concrete, 8 | Steel: DEF Steel, 5
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts trade/company/headcount from free text. 'Smith Concrete had 8 guys' → structured row. Fuzzy match company names to sub directory.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI extraction good; headcount sometimes ambiguous. ~10 sec to scan pre-filled table.

attributes.name: Work Performed (Structured)
attributes.example: Pour 3rd floor slab, Bldg B, Grid H-K | 42 CY | 85%
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM parses work descriptions into structured fields. Activity, location usually stated. % complete often missing. Quantities sometimes vague.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Most time-consuming field. AI saves ~50% vs. manual extraction. ~15–20 sec.

attributes.name: Equipment on Site
attributes.llmTechnique: Equipment mentions explicit: 'crane', 'pump', 'bobcat'. Standard vocabulary. NLP extraction with equipment type list.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Limited equipment types. Very reliable.

attributes.name: Issues / Delays Reported
attributes.example: Concrete delivery delayed 2 hrs. Rebar inspection failed — missing ties.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies issue sentences and extracts structured fields. Cause classification from 10 categories. Duration sometimes not quantified.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Extraction good; categorization needs human confirmation. ~10 sec.

attributes.name: Delay Cause Category
attributes.options: Weather | Material Delivery | Labor Shortage | Equipment | Inspection Failure | Design Issue | Owner Decision | Subcontractor | Permit | None Reported
attributes.llmTechnique: Given extracted issue text, classify into 10 categories. Some overlap (weather delays delivery). Few-shot with examples of each.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Pre-filled per issue. ~3 sec per issue to confirm.

attributes.name: Safety Observations
attributes.inputType: Table: Observation | Severity | Trade | Corrective Action
attributes.example: Harness compliance issue — 1 ironworker, corrected on site
attributes.llmTechnique: Safety language is distinctive: 'harness', 'PPE', 'violation', 'incident', 'near miss'. LLM extracts reliably. Severity classification simpler.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Safety language explicit. Severity needs review only on High/Critical.

attributes.name: Productivity Indicator
attributes.options: Above Expected | On Track | Below Expected | Significantly Behind | Weather Day
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can suggest based on crew count vs. work completed, but 'expected' requires knowing the plan. Without schedule context, unreliable.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Requires field judgment about what 'expected' means for this project/activity.

attributes.name: RFI / Issue Cross-Reference
attributes.options: Link daily report issues to RFIs, COs, safety incidents
attributes.whyItMatters: Connects field conditions to formal docs
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts explicit RFI refs. Semantic search matches issue descriptions against open RFI corpus. 'Rebar issue at Grid J' → find matching RFI.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Explicit refs reliable. Implicit matches need human confirmation. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Narrative Quality Score
attributes.example: Good — adequate detail on crews and work
attributes.whyItMatters: Identifies which supers write useful reports
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can assess length, structure, completeness heuristically. But calibrating 'Good' vs 'Excellent' requires knowing what good daily reports look like.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION initially. After 200+ scored reports, LLM can learn pattern → evolves to AI+Verify.

summary: 37 attributes per activity-day. The most granular schema — foundation for self-perform intelligence. High data volume: 12,000-18,000 records over 2-year portfolio.

attributes.name: Activity Record ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Composite key generated from project+date+trade+activity

attributes.name: Project
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Date
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: CSI Division
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from activity description in daily report. 'Poured 3rd floor slab' → Div 03. Consistent vocabulary in daily reports.

attributes.name: Activity Type
attributes.options: Standardized activities per division. Div 03: Formwork | Rebar | Concrete Placement | Finishing | Post-Tension. Div 05: Erection | Misc Metals | Decking. Div 22: Underground | Above-Grade | Trim | Testing.
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM maps daily report text to standardized activity list. 'Poured slab' → Concrete Placement. Few-shot with activity definitions per division.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Pre-built activity vocabulary per division. High accuracy on common activities.

attributes.name: Activity Subcategory
attributes.options: Further specificity. Concrete Placement: SOG | Elevated | Foundation | Wall | Column | Topping.
attributes.whyItMatters: Elevated vs SOG have different rates
attributes.llmTechnique: Cascaded: given Activity=Concrete Placement, classify sub from text. '3rd floor slab' → Elevated. 'Foundation pour' → Foundation.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI pre-fills; super confirms. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Building / Location
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts location references from daily report text. 'Building B, 3rd floor at grid H-K.' Parsing is text-dependent.

attributes.name: Self-Perform or Subcontractor
attributes.llmTechnique: Match crew/company from daily report against self-perform trades list + sub directory. Most firms know which trades they self-perform.

attributes.name: Total Labor Hours
attributes.options: Total worker-hours on this activity today
attributes.example: 72 LH (1 foreman x8 + 6 journeymen x8 + 2 apprentices x8)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts headcount × hours from daily report. '8-man crew, 8-hour day' → 64 LH. Cross-validate with timecard data if available.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Critical number — errors corrupt all rate calculations. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Crew Composition
attributes.inputType: Structured: Foremen | Journeymen | Apprentices | Operators | Laborers
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts when daily report specifies: '1 foreman, 6 journeymen, 2 apprentices.' Often not broken down this granularly in narrative.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Often needs human to fill in from knowledge of typical crew makeup. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Hours by Type
attributes.inputType: Structured: Productive | Waiting | Travel | Rework | Safety | Weather
attributes.example: Productive: 58 hrs | Waiting (pump delay): 6 | Rework: 4 | Safety: 4
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can identify disruption mentions and estimate time from text. But precise hour breakdown requires field knowledge not in the narrative.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI flags disruption mentions as starting point. Human allocates hours based on field knowledge.

attributes.name: Overtime / Shift
attributes.options: Straight Time | OT (hrs) | Double | Night | Weekend | Split
attributes.example: Straight Time — all 72 hours at regular rate
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM checks daily report for OT mentions. Cross-ref with timecard data. Weekend/night dates detectable from calendar.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Usually determinable from timecard data or date. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Crew Continuity
attributes.options: Same Crew (3+ days) | Partial Turnover (1-2 new) | Significant (3+ new / >30%) | New Crew | Mixed
attributes.example: Same Crew — team on 3rd floor pours for 2 weeks
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can compare today's crew list to yesterday's if both are extracted. But crew stability assessment requires field knowledge.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. Critical for productivity analysis. 20-40% impact makes this worth human attention.

attributes.name: Quantity Installed
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts quantities from daily report. 'Placed 42 CY concrete.' Numeric extraction with unit identification.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Critical number. Extraction good when stated explicitly. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Unit of Measure
attributes.options: CY | SF | LF | EA | TON | SQ | LB
attributes.llmTechnique: Determined by activity type. Concrete = CY. Drywall = SF. Conduit = LF. Standardized mapping table.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 5%. Deterministic once activity type is known.

attributes.name: Production Rate (calculated)
attributes.options: Quantity / Total LH = Units per LH
attributes.llmTechnique: Simple division once quantity and hours are validated.

attributes.name: Productive Rate (adjusted)
attributes.llmTechnique: Division using productive hours subset.
attributes.humanRole: None — depends on Hours by Type accuracy

attributes.name: Estimated Production Rate
attributes.llmTechnique: Pull from estimate labels (Attribute #19 Cost Breakdown). Match activity to estimated rate. Requires estimate to be labeled first.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Mapping from estimate to field activity. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Daily Production vs Plan
attributes.whyItMatters: Real-time earned value at activity level
attributes.llmTechnique: Auto-calc once both rates validated.

attributes.name: Cumulative Production Efficiency
attributes.options: Running total: Cumulative LH Spent / LH Earned
attributes.llmTechnique: Rolling calculation across activity days.

attributes.name: Weather Impact
attributes.options: No Impact | Minor (<10%) | Moderate (10-30%) | Severe (half-day+) | Full Weather Day
attributes.llmTechnique: Cross-ref weather data (temp, wind, precip) against trade-specific thresholds. Concrete finishing >95F → Moderate. Steel erection >25mph → Severe.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI applies rule-based thresholds. Human overrides for local context. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Access / Congestion
attributes.options: Open | Moderate (1-2 trades) | Heavy (multiple) | Restricted | Occupied
attributes.example: Moderate — steel crew south end, concrete north end
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can count trades from daily report crew section. But 'congestion' is a spatial judgment from being on site.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows crew count as context. Super assesses actual congestion.

attributes.name: Material Availability
attributes.options: All Available | Minor Shortage | Material Delay (waiting) | Wrong Material | Defective
attributes.example: Material Delay — pump arrived 45 min late (6 LH lost)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts material delay mentions from daily report. 'Pump late by 45 min' → Material Delay. Duration extraction.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Extraction reliable when mentioned. Absence of mention → 'All Available.' ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Equipment Performance
attributes.options: All Operational | Minor Issue | Breakdown (hrs lost) | Not Available | Insufficient
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM flags equipment issues from daily report. 'Crane down for 2 hours' → Breakdown. Absence = All Operational.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Similar to material — flag mentions. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Design / Information Issues
attributes.options: No Issues | Pending RFI (proceeding) | Open RFI (paused) | Conflict Discovered | ASI Received (rework) | Incomplete Info
attributes.example: Open RFI — RFI-0247 prevented waterproofing at parapet
attributes.whyItMatters: THE CRITICAL CROSS-REFERENCE to RFI cost
attributes.llmTechnique: Cross-ref open RFIs for this project + trade + location against daily report issues. Flag matching. 'Waiting on RFI' → Open RFI.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. System suggests matches; human confirms linkage. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Disruption Events (structured)
attributes.example: Pump late: 6 LH | Rebar inspection fail: 4 LH | Safety stand-down: 4 LH
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts disruption events from daily report narrative. Duration estimation from text. Cross-references to other doc types.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all events. AI extracts; human confirms duration estimates and cause categories. ~15 sec.

attributes.name: Disruption Cause Categories
attributes.options: Design Issue | Drawing Conflict | ASI Change | Owner Change | Material Delay | Equipment | Weather | Rework-Own | Rework-Design | Quality Rejection | Safety | Labor Shortage | Trade Stacking | Prerequisite | Permit | Access | Other
attributes.llmTechnique: Given extracted event, classify cause from 18 categories. 'Pump late' → Material Delay. 'Inspection fail' → Quality Rejection.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. AI classifies; human confirms or reclassifies. ~5 sec per event.

attributes.name: Total Disruption Hours
attributes.llmTechnique: Sum from disruption event table.

attributes.name: Disruption Cost
attributes.llmTechnique: Multiplication. Rate from labor rate table.

attributes.name: Responsible Party
attributes.options: GC | Subcontractor | Owner | Design Team | Supplier | Regulatory | Weather | Shared
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can suggest based on cause category. Material Delay → Supplier. Rework-Own → GC. But some are judgment calls.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI maps cause→party as suggestion. Human confirms. Politically sensitive on some attributions.

attributes.name: Days on This Activity
attributes.options: Consecutive days this crew on this activity
attributes.llmTechnique: Count from activity record sequence.

attributes.name: Learning Curve Phase
attributes.options: Startup (1-3) | Ramp-Up (3-7) | Peak | Sustained | Declining | Reset
attributes.example: Peak Production — crew on 3rd floor 8 days, 40-45 CY/day
attributes.llmTechnique: AI can correlate days-on-activity with rate trend. But 'Peak' vs 'Sustained' vs 'Declining' requires field context.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI shows rate trend graph as context. Human classifies phase.

attributes.name: Productivity Trend (7-day)
attributes.llmTechnique: Statistical calculation from rate series.

attributes.name: Rework Today?
attributes.options: No Rework | Minor (<1 hr) | Moderate (1-4 hrs) | Major (>4 hrs) | Full Redo
attributes.example: Minor — form blowout at Grid J, 4 LH
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM flags rework mentions in daily report. 'Form blowout', 'torn out and redone', 'patched'. Duration extraction.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Rework language distinctive. Duration needs confirmation. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Rework Cause
attributes.options: Workmanship Error | Design Error | Material Defect | Inspection Failure | Damage by Others | Owner Change | Coordination Failure
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can suggest from rework description. 'Form blowout' → likely Workmanship. 'Built per wrong drawing' → Design Error.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI suggests; human decides. Attribution matters for cost recovery and training.

attributes.name: Rework Labor Hours
attributes.options: LH spent on rework (subset of total)
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from daily report if stated. '2 workers spent 2 hours fixing forms' → 4 LH.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Extraction when stated explicitly. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Rework Cost
attributes.llmTechnique: Multiplication + material waste estimate.

summary: 23 attributes across incidents, observations, and inspections. Safety language is distinctive — LLMs classify well. Root cause and predictive flags stay human.

attributes.name: Report ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Project / Date / Time
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct API pull

attributes.name: Report Type
attributes.options: Near Miss | First Aid | Recordable Injury | Lost Time | Fatality | Property Damage | Environmental | Safety Observation | Stop Work
attributes.llmTechnique: Report titles and descriptions have clear type signals. 'Near miss' is stated explicitly. Recordable vs First Aid from injury description.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Very distinctive language per type.

attributes.name: Severity
attributes.example: Medium — fall hazard from non-compliant harness at 12'
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM assesses from description. Height + no harness → Medium/High. 'Corrected immediately' signals lower. Elevated heights + imminent danger → Critical.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Stakes are high on severity classification. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Trade Involved
attributes.llmTechnique: Extract trade/company from report. 'Ironworker from DEF Steel' → match to directory. Trade usually stated in safety reports.

attributes.name: Hazard Category
attributes.options: Fall Protection | Struck-By | Caught-In | Electrical | Excavation | Scaffolding | Crane | Housekeeping | PPE | Fire/Hot Work | Confined Space | Silica | Heat | Vehicle | Chemical | Other
attributes.llmTechnique: 16 categories with distinctive vocabulary. 'Harness', 'lanyard', 'guardrail' → Fall Protection. 'Trench', 'excavation' → Excavation. Few-shot with 3 examples per category.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Very reliable — safety vocabulary is specific.

attributes.name: Activity at Time of Event
attributes.example: Steel erection — connecting beams at 3rd floor
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts activity description from report. Maps to standardized activity list. Usually explicit in safety reports.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Pre-filled activity description; human confirms standardized mapping. ~3 sec.

attributes.name: Root Cause
attributes.options: Lack of Training | Inadequate PPE | Improper Procedure | Equipment Failure | Housekeeping | Supervisor Failure | Worker Behavior | Design/Engineering | Environmental | Inadequate Planning | Multiple
attributes.example: Worker Behavior — experienced ironworker chose convenience over compliance
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM can suggest based on description, but root cause requires understanding systemic vs individual factors. 'Worker removed harness' could be Worker Behavior OR Supervisor Failure.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. AI suggestion shown. Human determines root cause with context. Every correction logged.

attributes.name: Corrective Action Taken
attributes.options: Verbal Warning | Written | Re-Training | Stand-Down | Equipment Repair | Engineering Control | PPE Provided | Procedure Revised | Sub Notified | No Action (positive)
attributes.example: Verbal warning + toolbox talk for steel crew
attributes.llmTechnique: Corrective actions usually stated in report. LLM extracts and classifies. 'Verbal warning issued' → Verbal Warning. Multi-action reports handled.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Pre-filled from report text. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Weather / Environmental Conditions
attributes.llmTechnique: Pull from daily report weather data or weather API for that date/location. LLM identifies if weather was contributing factor.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 5%. Mostly automated from weather data.

attributes.name: Cross-Reference to Daily Report
attributes.options: Link to daily report and production record
attributes.llmTechnique: Auto-match by date + project. Find daily report entries mentioning same incident. Link to production disruption records.

attributes.name: Recurring Pattern
attributes.options: First Instance | Recurring-Same Sub | Recurring-Same Trade | Recurring-Same Activity | Recurring-Same Conditions | Known Industry Pattern
attributes.example: Recurring-Same Trade — 3rd harness observation on ironworkers in 6 weeks
attributes.llmTechnique: After corpus: system searches for similar incidents by trade/hazard/conditions and flags potential patterns. Before: human knowledge only.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION initially. System-assisted after 500+ labeled incidents.

attributes.name: Predictive Risk Flag
attributes.options: Condition combinations that predict incidents + Flag for Pre-Task Planning
attributes.example: Flag: YES — 'Steel erection + wind >15mph + new crew = highest-risk combo'
attributes.llmTechnique: After substantial labeled data: system identifies statistical correlations between conditions and incidents. Human validates and flags.
attributes.humanRole: FULL DECISION. The highest-value safety label. Requires pattern recognition + field experience.

attributes.name: Inspection Type
attributes.options: Structural Special | Concrete | Steel | MEP | Fire/Life Safety | Envelope | Roofing | Code/AHJ | Punchlist | Commissioning
attributes.llmTechnique: Inspection reports have clear type identification. 'Pre-pour inspection' → Concrete. 'Welding inspection' → Steel.

attributes.name: Result
attributes.options: Pass | Pass with Comments | Conditional Pass | Fail-Minor | Fail-Major | Incomplete
attributes.example: Fail — Minor (missing rebar ties at Grid J)
attributes.llmTechnique: Result usually stated explicitly in inspection report. 'Approved' → Pass. 'Failed — correct and re-inspect' → Fail-Minor.
attributes.humanRole: Spot-check 10%. Severity (Minor vs Major) may need verification.

attributes.name: Failure Reason
attributes.options: Workmanship | Material | Missing Documentation | Incomplete Work | Not Per Submittal | Code Violation | Coordination | N/A
attributes.example: Workmanship — rebar ties missing at 3 intersections
attributes.llmTechnique: If failed, LLM reads inspector comments to classify reason. 'Ties missing' → Workmanship. 'Not per approved submittal' → Not Per Submittal.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all failure reasons. Correct classification important for trade quality scoring. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Trade Responsible
attributes.options: Sub or self-perform whose work was inspected
attributes.llmTechnique: Inspection report identifies whose work was inspected. Match to sub directory.

attributes.name: Inspection Location / Area
attributes.whyItMatters: Location pattern for punch list prediction

attributes.name: Related Punch List Items
attributes.whyItMatters: Inspection failure to punch list linkage

summary: 32 attributes across Meeting Minutes, Pay Applications, Correspondence/Notices, and Punch List/Closeout. Administrative backbone completing the intelligence picture.

attributes.name: Meeting ID / Date
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Meeting Type
attributes.options: OAC | Progress/Status | Coordination | Pre-Construction | Safety | Scheduling | Design Review | Commissioning | Closeout | Special
attributes.llmTechnique: Meeting titles are descriptive: 'OAC Meeting #14', 'Weekly Coordination Meeting.' Very reliable classification.

attributes.name: Attendees (Key Roles)
attributes.options: Owner rep, architect, GC PM, key subs
attributes.example: Owner: J. Smith | Arch: L. Chen | GC: J. Morrison | Mech: B. Torres
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts names and roles from attendee list. Match to project directory.

attributes.name: Action Items Count
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM counts action items from meeting minutes. Usually structured: 'Action:', numbered lists, or tables.

attributes.name: Action Items by Owner
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts responsible party per action item and aggregates. Party identification from text.

attributes.name: Decisions Documented
attributes.example: Owner approved alternate ceiling tile (~$8K deductive). Architect to issue revised MEP drawings by 4/15.
attributes.whyItMatters: Decision extraction; 'When did owner approve X?'
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies decision language: 'approved', 'decided', 'agreed to', 'directed'. Extracts with context.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all decisions. Important for formal record. AI extraction is good starting point. ~12 sec.

attributes.name: Open Issues Discussed
attributes.example: 4 open from prior; 2 resolved, 2 carried forward
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM identifies 'old business', 'open items', 'carried forward' sections. Counts and summarizes.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Meeting minutes structure varies by firm. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: RFI/CO/Schedule Cross-References
attributes.example: Discussed: RFI-0247, CO-015 (pending), Schedule: 2-week delay Bldg B enclosure
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts explicit document references (RFI-###, CO-###) from meeting text. High accuracy on explicit refs.

attributes.name: Pay App Number / Period
attributes.options: Pay app number and billing period
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Scheduled Value (original SOV)
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Current Contract Value
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Billed This Period
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Billed to Date (%)
attributes.llmTechnique: Division

attributes.name: Retainage Held
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Payment Received Date
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Days to Payment
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic

attributes.name: Billing vs Earned Value
attributes.options: Overbilled | On Track (within 5%) | Underbilled | Front-Loaded | Disputed Items
attributes.example: On Track — 62.3% billed vs 60% schedule complete
attributes.llmTechnique: Compare billed % to schedule % complete (if available from P6). Simple arithmetic + threshold. 'Disputed Items' from text.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. AI calculates over/under. Human confirms interpretation. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Disputed / Held Items
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM reads pay app notes/correspondence for held items. Extracts amounts and reasons.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Held items are usually documented. ~5 sec.

attributes.name: Correspondence ID
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Type
attributes.options: Formal Letter | Notice of Claim | Notice of Delay | Notice of Changed Conditions | Owner Directive | Architect Response | Transmittal | Stop Work | Cure Notice | Demand Letter | Cert. of Subst. Completion | General Correspondence
attributes.example: Notice of Delay — formal notification of schedule impact
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies from document title + content. 'Notice of Delay' explicitly stated. Transmittals have distinctive format. 12 clear categories.

attributes.name: Sent By / Sent To
attributes.example: From: HCB (J. Morrison) | To: HKS, cc: Owner
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts from header/signature.

attributes.name: Contractual Notice?
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM cross-refs document type against contract clause requirements. If 'Notice of Delay' and contract requires delay notice → Yes-Required. Clause citation from contract labels.
attributes.humanRole: Verify all. Notice compliance has legal consequences. AI flags; human confirms clause reference. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Notice Timeliness
attributes.options: Timely (within window) | Late (sent but outside window) | Not Sent (required but missed) | N/A
attributes.example: Timely — sent within 14-day window per Article 8.3.1
attributes.llmTechnique: System calculates: triggering event date + contractual notice window vs. notice send date. Requires contract clause labels for window period.
attributes.humanRole: VERIFY ALL. Missed notices = forfeited claim rights. AI calculates timeline; human confirms trigger date. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Related Documents
attributes.options: RFIs, COs, contract clauses, daily reports referenced
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM extracts explicit document references from letter text. High accuracy on explicit refs.

attributes.name: Punch List ID / Area
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct pull

attributes.name: Total Punch Items
attributes.llmTechnique: Direct count

attributes.name: Items by Trade
attributes.example: Mechanical: 14 | Electrical: 8 | Drywall: 9 | Painting: 7 | Flooring: 5 | GC: 4
attributes.llmTechnique: Procore assigns trades to punch items. System aggregates by trade. If not tagged, LLM classifies from item description.

attributes.name: Items by Severity
attributes.inputType: Structured: Life Safety | Functional | Aesthetic | Documentation
attributes.example: Life Safety: 2 (fire caulking) | Functional: 18 | Aesthetic: 22 | Documentation: 5
attributes.llmTechnique: LLM classifies each punch item into 4 severity categories. 'Fire caulking missing' → Life Safety. 'Paint touch-up' → Aesthetic.
attributes.humanRole: Verify Life Safety items — zero tolerance for misclassification. Others spot-check. ~8 sec.

attributes.name: Days to Complete
attributes.llmTechnique: Date arithmetic

attributes.name: Back-Charges Issued?
attributes.example: Yes — $4,200 to mechanical for incomplete insulation (6 items)
attributes.llmTechnique: System checks Sage for back-charges linked to this project/sub. LLM reads punch list notes for back-charge mentions.

attributes.name: Warranty Items (post-closeout)
attributes.example: 2 warranty calls — HVAC balancing (Div 23), roof leak at parapet (Div 07)
attributes.llmTechnique: Warranty items arrive via separate correspondence/calls. System can't auto-detect. Human enters as they occur.
attributes.humanRole: FULL ENTRY. Ongoing data entry as warranty issues arise.

attributes.name: Closeout Document Completeness
attributes.inputType: Checklist: O&M | Warranties | As-Builts | Training | Attic Stock | Lien Waivers | Consent | CO
attributes.example: O&M: Complete | Warranties: 2 of 8 outstanding (mech, elec) | As-Builts: Complete
attributes.llmTechnique: System checks Procore closeout module for document status. Aggregates by type. Flags outstanding items.
attributes.humanRole: Verify. Procore may not track all closeout docs. Human supplements. ~5 sec.

summary: 37 fields. Financial backbone connecting accounting to project intelligence. Contains budget vs actual cost data at the cost-code level.
